title: Harden installation entrypoints, core gates, localization, and compatibility

riskAssessment.reason: Changes affect public onboarding, lifecycle coverage gates, localization, and compatibility CI.

executionDecision.reason: The review provides concrete defects and acceptance criteria.

preReviewWarnings: Review CI runtime cost and ensure public compatibility claims match executable jobs.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All primary README installation commands create an auditable adoption record.
Japanese examples contain no untranslated instructional sentences and terminology is consistent.
A conventional make test entrypoint runs the project test suite.
Lifecycle-critical scripts gain direct failure-path tests and materially stronger per-file coverage floors.
Static type checking covers all governance scripts that currently pass strict checking.
Compatibility documentation and CI distinguish verified stacks from preset-only stacks without overclaiming.
All repository quality and AI governance checks pass.
guidelines: Keep installation commands executable and consistent across languages.
Treat compatibility as evidence-based and avoid claims beyond CI coverage.
Preserve fail-closed lifecycle behavior while increasing test depth.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly requested repository-wide hardening in this review response.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
